texto: Don't be afraid to write. I think a lot of writers of all ages have a fear that they aren't any good at writing. That fear is normal. I feel it. Most writers who care about what they are writing feel it. When you feel like you're not good enough, write anyway. It's just a pesky bug that's whispering lies. The best way to swat the bug is with your pen.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tanya Anderson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Don't be afraid to write. I think a lot of writers of all ages have a fear that they aren't any g…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, fear, insecurity pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de insecurity e lies.
